source_legislation: NIST AI 100-1 (AI Risk Management Framework 1.0, January 2023)

provisions.description: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively

sub_provisions.description: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented
Processes and procedures are in place for oversight and human intervention throughout the AI lifecycle
Processes and procedures are in place for decommissioning and phasing out AI systems safely and in a manner that does not increase risks or harms

evaluation.message: At least one governance_policy record is required — GOVERN 1 mandates documented policies, processes, and procedures for AI risk management
Governance policy records should include attached policy documents demonstrating established organizational AI governance
Governance policy records should be linked to subject entities to establish which AI systems are covered by the governance framework

provisions.description: Context is established and understood, including the intended deployment setting, legal and regulatory requirements, and the broader socio-technical environment in which the AI system will operate

sub_provisions.description: Intended purposes, potentially beneficial uses, context of use, and deployment setting are documented
Organizational risk tolerances are determined and documented

evaluation.message: At least one risk_register record is required — MAP 1 mandates risk identification and contextual analysis of the AI system deployment environment
Risk register records should be linked to a subject entity for traceability of system-context risk mapping
Risk register records should categorize risks per NIST trustworthiness characteristics (validity, reliability, safety, security, accountability, transparency, explainability, privacy, fairness)

provisions.description: Categorization of the AI system is performed, including data requirements and characteristics for training, validation, and testing datasets

sub_provisions.description: The specific tasks and methods used to implement the tasks that the AI system will support are defined
Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations are identified and documented

evaluation.message: At least one dataset_card record is required — MAP 2 mandates documentation of training, validation, and testing datasets including characteristics and limitations
At least one data_provenance record is required — MAP 2 requires documentation of data lineage and acquisition for categorization
Dataset card records should be linked to dataset entities for proper data governance traceability

provisions.description: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied for measuring AI risks and trustworthiness characteristics relevant to the AI system's deployment context

sub_provisions.description: Approaches and metrics for measurement of AI risks enumerated during the MAP function are selected for implementation
Internal experts who did not serve as combinedjudges of the AI system's development or deployment are used for independent review

evaluation.message: At least one evaluation_report record is required — MEASURE 1 mandates documented evaluation metrics, test conditions, and results for AI trustworthiness assessment
Evaluation reports should include quantified metrics for measuring AI risks and trustworthiness per MEASURE 1.1
Evaluation reports should document the evaluation methodology to ensure reproducibility and independent verification per MEASURE 1.3

provisions.description: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output from the MAP and MEASURE functions are prioritized, responded to, and managed

sub_provisions.description: A determination is made as to whether the AI system achieves its intended purpose and stated objectives and whether its development or deployment should proceed
Responses to the AI risks deemed high priority, as identified by the MAP function, are developed, planned, and documented

evaluation.message: At least one risk_treatment record is required — MANAGE 1 mandates documented risk responses including treatment strategy, implementation timeline, and responsible party
Risk treatment records should specify the treatment_type (eliminate, reduce, mitigate, accept, transfer) per MANAGE 1.3 prioritized response documentation
Risk treatment records should be linked to a subject entity for traceability of risk management actions to specific AI systems

provisions.description: Incidents and errors are communicated and documented, risk treatments are monitored and their effectiveness is assessed

sub_provisions.description: Post-deployment AI system monitoring plans are implemented, including mechanisms for capturing and evaluating input from users and other relevant AI actors
Measurable activities for continual improvements are integrated into AI system updates and include regular engagement with interested parties

evaluation.message: At least one incident_report record is required — MANAGE 4 mandates documented incident response, root cause analysis, corrective actions, and communication of errors
Incident reports should include a severity classification for prioritized response per MANAGE 4.1
Incident reports should be linked to a subject entity to establish which AI system experienced the incident
Incident reports should document corrective_actions taken per MANAGE 4.2 continual improvement requirements
